issues.title: UI consistency sweep after asset promotion + office-sim integration (level-2 visual pass)
issues.body:
Pip's forward-looking concern (2026-07-19), captured so it isn't lost.

## The two levels of the art pipeline
- **Level 1 (building now):** the review app's Keep/Reroll wiring PROMOTES asset files into `godot/assets/` and feeds Rerolls into the next generation wave. Makes assets *available*.
- **Level 2 (this issue):** the game UI must actually *reference and render* the promoted assets, the office simulator gets folded onto a real game screen, and then -- crucially -- a CONSISTENCY SWEEP over ALL UI elements.

## The sweep
Once the new assets are promoted + the office-sim is integrated + round-2 UI improvements land, audit every UI element/screen for visual coherence against the locked style: warm-grime + heavy-outline heft + deeper shadow; palette from `docs/art/palette.json`; consistent icon stroke/sizing; panel-frame treatment; spacing; the doom-as-additive-layer intensity rules. Goal: old and new assets don't clash; the whole game reads as one visual system.

## Sequencing (blocked-until)
Do the sweep AFTER: (a) assets promoted (level 1), (b) office-sim folded into a screen (see #663 + the OfficeFloor integration task), (c) round-2 UI improvements. Approach: inventory all UI elements -> map each to current vs intended asset -> flag inconsistencies -> apply the style guide uniformly.

Not blocking the v0.11.0 alpha release (which ships with current UI). This is the visual-coherence capstone for the art push.

issues.title: Playtest v0.11.0: defeat-pacing drag + hiring pipeline feels gappy (coalesce/de-branch)
issues.body:
From Pip's live v0.11.0 playtest (2026-07-18). Post-alpha design work; NOT release-blocking (the end-game quit is separate and release-blocking -- see branch fix/endgame-quit-score-post).

## 1. Defeat pacing is a drag
Getting defeated took too long / felt low-stakes. Complements the existing "no loss in first ~6 turns" heuristic: the difficulty curve needs a genuine FAILURE RAMP after the safe early window, so the mid/late game has escalating stakes instead of a slow grind to the end. Tune the doom/finance/reputation pressure so a losing run resolves in a satisfying arc, not a war of attrition.

## 2. Hire -> onboard -> equip -> mentor pipeline feels incomplete
The pipeline doesn't read as fully there yet -- "a few little gaps in logics", and the onboard/equip/mentor stages don't land clearly. Pip's framing: "more coalescing and de-branching needs to happen" -- consolidate overlapping/parallel paths, and cut or finish half-wired branches so the flow is one coherent legible sequence rather than several partial ones.

Overall dev read: "internally about where I expect it to be" + "great progress" -- these are refinement items, not a teardown.

issues.title: Comprehensive upgrades system overhaul
issues.body:
## Summary
The 'upgrades' section needs a comprehensive overhaul tied into useful office and operational expenses.

## Context
From playtesting session 2025-11-27. Current upgrades feel disconnected from actual gameplay needs.

## Proposed Categories
### Office & Facilities
- Workspace quality (desks, chairs, lighting)
- Kitchen and amenities
- Meeting rooms
- Security systems
- Location upgrade (move to better office)

### Equipment & Infrastructure
- Compute clusters
- Development tools
- Network infrastructure
- Backup systems

### Software & Systems
- Accounting software (see #475)
- Project management tools
- Communication platforms
- HR systems

### Research Tools
- Datasets
- Specialized hardware
- Cloud credits
- Academic subscriptions

## Mechanics
- Each upgrade has:
  - Upfront cost
  - Ongoing maintenance
  - Tangible gameplay benefit
  - Clear prerequisite chain

## Integration
- Tied to budget management
- Visible in office representation
- Affects researcher productivity/morale
- Strategic choices: what to prioritize?

## Related Systems
- Office outfitting (#467)
- Finance management (#475)
- Personnel system

## Priority
High - core gameplay loop improvement

issues.title: Navigation audit + universal principle (ESC/back consistency, mirrored toggle keys, unreachable Employee screen)
issues.body: Playtest 85ad582: nav inconsistencies. Employee screen has no back-to-main except a button — **ESC there opens the game menu** instead of returning to main UI. **No native path to the Employee screen** from main UI (needs an affordance/icon). Establish a PRINCIPLE for consistent coverage sweeps: mirrored toggle keys (a key that opens a panel closes it) + consistent ESC/back (returns to previous/main, not game menu, unless top-level). Audit all screens/overlays against it.

issues.title: Nomenclature + pacing: strategic-options unlock expressed at month grain; align fanfare hero
issues.body:
Pip 2026-07-21, playtesting the new backdrop build: 'dial in the strategic options unlock with that hero screen... game turns are counted through planning, and we want strategic models to kick in around turn 10 in the game, so that's nearly a year into the game on a month by month basis. Tightening up this nomenclature is important.'

Scope: (a) audit where strategic-options unlock timing is defined (turn counts vs months -- ADR-0009's layer-not-regrain means 'turn 10' may mean 10 planning phases ~= 10 months); express unlock pacing in MONTHS in data + docs; (b) align the fanfare_strategic_moves hero moment with the intended ~1-year-in beat; (c) this is a concrete instance of the turn/month nomenclature cleanup flagged in VOICE_AND_LEGIBILITY_SCAN (confusing-weird item 2). Ties: ADR-0009, #757 conditional-art notes. Size: S audit + ruling.

issues.title: Session agenda: Friday 2026-07-24 (decisions + conversations queue)
issues.body:
Lossless capture of everything queued for Pip's Friday session, accumulated 2026-07-20/21. Check items off or re-schedule; this issue closes when drained.

CONVERSATIONS (design, ~20 min each):
- [ ] Doom-color spec: secondary/tertiary colour arrangements per doom level/band -- feeds the dial gradient (icon round 2), banner_doom_rising variants (held conservative pending this), and completes docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md. Pip: 'we can get pretty specific about this.'
- [ ] Fonts + wordmark: typography direction for the type_dial_paren logo (LaTeX/Bayes P(doom) studies incoming on icon round 2), in-game fonts (godot/assets/fonts/ is empty), wordmark refinement.
- [ ] Portrait system ratification: per-archetype VARIANT POOLS with random gender/appearance assignment (ruled directionally 2026-07-21; dossier-v2 set promoted as current consistent set, painterly kept as pool stock). Decide the assignment mechanism + how variants map to save/determinism (seeded assignment?).

DECISIONS (one-liners):
- [ ] Public-alpha distribution channel (docs/ROADMAP.md 'Decision owed' section: website-direct / itch / self-hosted web build)
- [ ] Art masters policy: Git LFS for 1024 masters vs 512/WebP-canonical (masters currently safe in G:/tmp/pdoom1-art-masters/)
- [ ] Rival roster naming pass (Pip's voice; WORLD_AND_LORE mismatch)

WORK WITH PIP IN THE LOOP:
- [ ] #735 leaderboard enable: real endpoint + token (Pip-only), hardening minimums
- [ ] Playtest the merged visual stack (theme #749, backdrop wiring #756-lane, icon promotions) in one build
- [ ] v0.11.0 release-notes voice pass (also fixes the bug-reporter overpromise)
- [ ] Grant: Thursday grantmaking.ai submission should be DONE before Friday -- if not, it is dead; Manifund page + Screen Australia Emerging Gamemakers (closes 27 Aug) next
- [ ] Icon round 2 + round 3 verdict passes in the upgraded gallery (evil laser cat, Dept of Documentation seals, dial, logo studies; icon/banner/portrait re-rolls)

Standing context: docs/strategy/SUCCESS_PREMORTEM_2026-07-20.md section 5 decisions log; DQ_INDEX.md for the design register.

issues.title: art: app-icon candidates + settings-bg alternatives (fast pass)
issues.body:
Fast-pass **stopgap** candidate set ahead of the afternoon designer hire -- quantity + variety over polish. Do not merge; this is for Pip's fast-pass iteration.

## Review here first
**Contact sheet: `tools/art_review/icon_pass_2026-07-21.html`** (open locally; relative image paths, click any icon for the 1024px master, small pair under each icon is the 32/48px render).

## Deliverable 1 -- app-icon candidates (15)
For the Windows-export application icon (SmartScreen fix, issue #732) + logo stopgap. All in the locked palette (`docs/art/PALETTE_AND_DOOM_INTENSITY.md`), rendered 32 -> 1024px so 32x32 legibility is checkable directly.
- **Office cat (3):** `cat_face`, `cat_face_doom` (soured-red worried variant), `cat_sitting`
- **Doom gauge/dial (4):** `dial_clock` (doomsday-clock), `dial_half`, `dial_ring`, `dial_radar`
- **Bureaucratic seal/stamp (4):** `seal_star`, `seal_hourglass` ("PAPERWORK THAT MIGHT / SAVE THE WORLD"), `seal_check`, `seal_red`
- **P(Doom) typographic (4):** `type_wordmark`, `type_amber`, `type_mono` (best at 32px), `type_dial_paren` (gauge inside the parens)

## Deliverable 2 -- settings background, 3 warmer alternatives (512x512)
Drop-in replacements for the sage/green `tex_painted_metal_panel_512` (the greenest kept ground), same peel + corner/edge-screw composition shifted warm: `warm_ochre`, `warm_terracotta` (warmest), `warm_bakelite` (cozy deep brown).

## Pipeline note / blocker
Intended pipeline was in-repo **gpt-image-1** (`tools/assets`), but **`OPENAI_API_KEY` was absent in every env scope** (no key file, no GEMINI key either), so it could not run. To still deliver real PNGs today without burning the parallel agent's pixellab credits on off-register pixel-art, these were generated **procedurally** (PIL/numpy, no API): `tools/art_review/gen_icon_candidates.py`, `tools/art_review/gen_settings_grounds.py`. Set `OPENAI_API_KEY` to run higher-fidelity gpt-image-1 versions (esp. the office cat, which benefits most from a generative model).

Spend: **$0.00**.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Dev overlays (DEV MODE + F3) can't read live game state; 29 runtime errors; nudge/advance-turn wiring
issues.body: Playtest 85ad582: DEV MODE (backslash) and F3 both report **'no active game state'** during a live game, so risk-pools + rival-labs sections are empty. F3 also shows **29 runtime errors** (all 'color white refcounted ...') + a perf tab (RAM/FPS) but no risk pools. DEV MODE resource +/- **nudge buttons may not be wired** to change state (verify). **Advance-turn** gives no visible feedback (works? overlay may cover the UI). Also: **backslash should CLOSE** dev mode (toggle). Design guard needed: dev-mode state pokes should NOT corrupt the verification/event log.

issues.title: chore: docs typographic ASCII cleanup (em-dashes, box-drawing) -- low priority
issues.body:
Follow-up from PR #751 (blocking no-emoji hook + godot/ ASCII purge). Remaining out of scope there: ~1282 em-dashes and box-drawing architecture diagrams across ~79 docs/ files. Convert to ASCII (em-dash -> --, box-drawing -> |/+/- ASCII trees) in one mechanical PR, then consider extending the no-emoji hook's full-ASCII coverage to docs/ (excluding docs/archive/ and docs/game-design/WORKSHOP_2_BACKLOG.md, which remain exempt historical files unless separately ruled).

Ruled low priority by Pip 2026-07-21; build in a quiet window OUTSIDE active-lane sessions -- mass mechanical edits across docs conflict with in-flight doc lanes, so schedule when no doc-touching lanes are running.

Size: S (mechanical, scripted).

issues.title: diag+harden: leaderboard crash diagnostic builds + build-verify/scene-smoke/asset-validation defenses
issues.body:
## Two jobs: (A) prepare to NAIL the release-only leaderboard segfault, (B) bulletproof against the class.

**The bug:** RELEASE-ONLY segfault while LOADING the leaderboard scene (game-over -> leaderboard), BEFORE `_ready` runs. Headless cannot reproduce it (verified: the new smoke test add-to-trees + runs `_ready` on the leaderboard scene cleanly). So this PR ships diagnostic builds + protocol + defenses, not a blind fix. **Do not merge** -- Pip runs the builds first.

### Part A -- the kill kit
- **3 verified-fresh diagnostic builds** produced by `tools/build_release.py` (each proven built-from-current-source; see below). Placed at stable paths under `builds/leaderboard-diag/{debug01,strip01,min01}/` (gitignored).
- `docs/LEADERBOARD_CRASH_DIAGNOSIS.md` -- **committed** ordered kill protocol (which build, exact `--verbose` command, what each result means).
- `leaderboard_screen.gd` -- `[LBTRACE-n]` stderr probes bracketing every `_ready` step.
- `leaderboard_screen_minimal.tscn` + `leaderboard_screen_min_stub.gd` -- reproducible minimal-scene fixture for the structure-vs-content bisection.
- `theme_manager.gd` -- drop the two dead `tex_cyan_ispf`/`tex_oxidized_copper` string refs (nothing `load()`s them).

### Part B -- bulletproofing (half the value)
- **`tools/build_release.py`** -- exports from a clean state (`rm -rf .godot`) then PROVES the pack is fresh via a unique **marker filename** (key finding: script string literals do NOT survive as grep-able text in this project's binary-tokenized `.gdc` export -- the old "grep for a printerr tag" advice is unreliable; filenames in the pack file table do survive). No build ships unverified.
- **`test_ui_scene_ready_smoke.gd`** -- add-to-tree + run `_ready` on every `scenes/ui` scene + standalone screen (the existing instantiate-only smoke never ran `_ready`). 21 scenes exercised.
- **`tools/validate_assets.py`** + `validate_assets_probe.gd` -- load every asset under `godot/assets`, fail loud on any that will not resolve.
- **`docs/POSTMORTEM_v0.11.0_ALPHA.md`** -- stale-export-cache trap, verify-the-pack discipline, the `all_resources` packing gotcha, and the headless render-gate limitation.

### Gate
Fast gate green: **534 tests, 0 failures (60/60 files)**. `build_release.py` dogfooded 3x (all PASS); `validate_assets.py` PASS.

### Best current hypothesis
Textures are almost certainly NOT the cause (dead string refs; welcome.tscn loads REAL textures fine as the boot scene). Leading suspect: the **`OptionButton` (SeedDropdown)** -- the one node type unique to this scene, which builds an internal PopupMenu/Window at instantiation, a plausible pre-`_ready` native-crash site in the release renderer. Build 3 (no OptionButton) is the direct test.

Headless cannot reproduce this class of crash; the human release-build playtest remains the final gate.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

issues.title: Art tooling: compare-and-contrast mode for kept assets (batch style reviews)
issues.body:
Pip 2026-07-21, from the round-2 sweep review: the hide-on-verdict viewer flow made clearing hundreds of cells easy ("way way way easier... I could now clear hundreds without getting tired"). Next capability: when doing batch STYLE reviews, an easy compare-and-contrast view of already-CHOSEN (kept) assets -- side-by-side across styles/palettes for refinement rounds, not just triage of new generations.

This edges toward a lightweight art-asset management layer (a CMS-ish view over art_source + verdicts.json). Keep it repo-resident HTML like the existing viewer (tools/art_review/build.py pattern), not an external service.

Not scheduled -- backlog until the next big style-refinement round makes it worth building.
